methods.name: FormatAnnotationValidity
methods.signature: public static string FormatAnnotationValidity(AnnotationBase val, string identifier = "Annotation")
methods.summary: If the annotation is invalid, return a string describing why.

params.name: val
params.doc: Value to format.

params.name: identifier
params.doc: Name used to refer to the value in the format.

returns.doc: A string describing why the annotation is invalid or String.Empty if the value is not invalid.

methods.name: FormatFilePath(String, Int32)
methods.signature: public static string FormatFilePath(string path, int maxLength)
methods.summary: Format a filepath so that it doesn't exceed a string length. If the filepath filename is already longer than maxLength it will be returned intact.

params.name: path
params.doc: Path for format.

params.name: maxLength
params.doc: Maximum number of characters to use.

returns.doc: The formatted path.

methods.name: FormatFilePath(String, Int32, Font)
methods.signature: public static string FormatFilePath(string path, int maxWidth, Font font)
methods.summary: Format a filepath so that it doesn't exceed width. If the filepath filename is already longer than maxLength it will be returned intact.

params.name: path
params.doc: Path to format.

params.name: maxWidth
params.doc: Maximum allowed width (in pixels)

params.name: font
params.doc: Font to use for measuring.

returns.doc: The formatted path.

params.name: val
params.doc: Value to format.

params.name: identifier
params.doc: Name used to refer to the value in the format.

params.name: testSingleton
params.doc: If true, Intervals that have no length are considered invalid.

params.name: testDecreasing
params.doc: If true, Intervals that are decreasing are considered invalid.

returns.doc: A string describing why the point is invalid or String.Empty if the value is not invalid.

methods.name: FormatString
methods.signature: public static string FormatString(string text, params Object[] args)
methods.summary: Replaces the format items in a string with the string representations of corresponding objects in a specified array.
methods.remarks: Formatting is done using invariant-culture formatting information.

params.name: text
params.doc: A composite format string.

params.name: args
params.doc: An object array that contains zero or more objects to format.

returns.doc: A copy of format in which the format items have been replaced by the string representation of the corresponding objects in args.

methods.name: TreatAsCollection
methods.summary: This method is used in Scripting components to determine whether a certain class was 'intended' as a collection or as a single piece of data .

params.name: obj
params.doc: Object to test.

returns.doc: True if we think the object was intended as a collection.
